title: acer pictum subsp. dissectum
intro: Acer pictum subsp. dissectum, also known as the Japanese maple or dissected Japanese maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with deeply cut, lacy foliage that is native to Japan. The medicinal properties of Acer pictum subsp. dissectum include its ability to act as an astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant agent. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function, as well as its potential to help lower cholesterol and blood pressure. Acer pictum subsp. dissectum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Japanese medicine, known as Kampo, to treat various health conditions.
